name: go_metro
explanation: go-metro is a passive TCP RTT sniffer (a Go binary using libpcap) that ships only with the legacy 64-bit Agent v5 DEB/RPM packages and is not available in Agent v6+. There is no upstream service to probe; instead the user nominates a network `interface` plus an `ips`/`hosts` allowlist of remote endpoints whose round-trip times should be measured. Like dns_check / http_check, the target is purely user intent (which peers do you care about RTT for?), not a discoverable on-host signal. Setup additionally requires `setcap cap_net_raw+ep` on the binary, so even runtime feasibility is host-state dependent.
notes: Pattern K (synthetic / user-intent probe), and additionally an Agent v5-only check that has no equivalent in current Agents. No check.py under datadog_checks/go-metro/ — the Python side is just a conf example for the embedded Go binary. Directory is hyphenated (`go-metro`); using underscored name `go_metro` to satisfy schema regex.
